figures.caption: Figure 2: Contamination score distribution. Red bars: contaminated behavior (CS ≥ 0.6); blue bars: genuine reasoning (CS < 0.6). The dashed line at 0.6 achieves perfect separation with no overlap.

Figure 3: Code diversity vs. gold BLEU for 9 problems. Three clusters emerge: contaminated (topleft, zero diversity, high gold match), contaminationflaw composite (bottom-left, zero diversity, low gold match), and genuine reasoning (right, high diversity).

Table 3: Per-problem statistics across all 45 trials (9 problems × 5 trials each). Diversity and Gold ḡ are computed via Eq. 2; Gold σg is the standard deviation of per-trial gold BLEU scores. Unique = number of distinct implementation approaches (manual inspection). Reas. = reasoning classifier output (NO = NO REASONING, FULL = FULL REASONING).

Table 1: CCV results for 9 SWE-bench Verified problems. Div., Gold, Time, Reas., and CS are computed from litemode trials (issue text only, no repository). Applied = patches successfully applied in separate full-mode Docker runs (python:3.11-slim). Rows above the mid-rule show contaminated behavior (CS ≥ 0.6); below show genuine reasoning (CS < 0.6). †Pre-labeled as contamination suspect; CCV reclassifies as genuine reasoning.
Figure 1: CCV pipeline. A benchmark problem is solved N times in session-isolated trials. Solution diversity and gold proximity are analyzed to produce contamination and test flaw scores.
